inputs.userGoal:
### A. Azure Workbook
가장 추천

- 여러 쿼리를 한 화면에 배치 가능
- 시간 선택, 필터, 차트 구성이 쉬움

### B. Azure Dashboard

- 간단히 pin 해서 운영용 대시보드 만들기 좋음 둘 다
subGoals: Operational excellence: provide a reusable Kafka operations view over existing HDInsight Kafka metrics in Log Analytics.
Minimal deployment risk: deploy only shared Workbook and shared Dashboard resources into an existing resource group.
Low-friction adoption: keep the Log Analytics workspace as an input so the assets can be deployed into any existing environment.

reasoning.whyChosen: Workbook and Dashboard resources are resource-group scoped and should live alongside the monitoring artifacts for the target HDInsight Kafka environment.
alternativesConsidered: Manual portal-only creation with no IaC artifacts
reasoning.tradeoffs: This plan assumes the resource group already exists and does not attempt to create or modify the monitored HDInsight cluster itself.

resources.name: wb-kafka-ops-shared

reasoning.whyChosen: Workbook is the best fit for multi-query Kafka operations views because it supports multiple KQL visuals, richer layout, and easier iteration (WAF Operational Excellence).
reasoning.tradeoffs: Workbook offers the richest experience but requires users to open the Workbook blade instead of seeing everything on the Azure portal home dashboard.

resources.dependencyReasoning: The shared workbook is deployed into the existing resource group and reads from an existing Log Analytics workspace.

resources.name: dash-kafka-ops

reasoning.whyChosen: A shared Azure Dashboard complements the workbook by surfacing the most important Kafka health charts directly in the portal and linking users into the richer workbook when deeper investigation is needed.
alternativesConsidered: Dashboard tiles pinned manually from the portal
reasoning.tradeoffs: Azure Dashboard layout is less flexible than Workbook layout, so only the highest-value charts are included there.

resources.dependencyReasoning: The dashboard is deployed into the same resource group and includes a deep link to the shared workbook.

overallReasoning.summary: The solution creates two deployable monitoring artifacts over an existing Log Analytics workspace: a shared Workbook for full Kafka operational analysis and a shared Dashboard for quick portal access to the most important Kafka health signals.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: The deployment intentionally avoids creating or changing the HDInsight cluster and assumes the required HDInsight monitor integration already populates HDInsightKafkaMetrics in the workspace.

plan.validation: The deployment is coherent because both resources are resource-group scoped, reference a single existing Log Analytics workspace, and use only read-only KQL against HDInsightKafkaMetrics.

references.title: Azure Monitor logs reference for HDInsight
